title: Mercedes Brignone

infobox.key: This article about an Italian stage actor is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Brignone&action=edit).

This article about an Italian television or movie actor is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Brignone&action=edit).

infobox.value: (18 May 1885 – 24 June 1967) was a Spanish-born Italian stage, film and television actress. She was the daughter of the actor [Giuseppe Brignone](/w/index.php?title=Giuseppe_Brignone&action=edit&redlink=1 "Giuseppe Brignone (page does not exist)"). She often played [divas](/wiki/Diva "Diva"), and appeared in numerous [silent films](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film") for [Milano Films](/wiki/Milano_Films "Milano Films") during the 1910s.[[1]](#cite_note-1) In later years she played largely [supporting roles](/wiki/Supporting_character "Supporting character"). She was the older sister of film director and actor [Guido Brignone](/wiki/Guido_Brignone "Guido Brignone") and the aunt of actress [Lilla Brignone](/wiki/Lilla_Brignone "Lilla Brignone").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian actress (1885–1967)

| Mercedes Brignone | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | (1885-05-18)18 May 1885  [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid "Madrid"), Spain |
| Died | 24 June 1967(1967-06-24) (aged 82)  [Milan](/wiki/Milan "Milan"), Italy |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 1912–1965 (film) |

**Mercedes Brignone** (18 May 1885 – 24 June 1967) was a Spanish-born Italian stage, film and television actress. She was the daughter of the actor [Giuseppe Brignone](/w/index.php?title=Giuseppe_Brignone&action=edit&redlink=1 "Giuseppe Brignone (page does not exist)"). She often played [divas](/wiki/Diva "Diva"), and appeared in numerous [silent films](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film") for [Milano Films](/wiki/Milano_Films "Milano Films") during the 1910s.[[1]](#cite_note-1) In later years she played largely [supporting roles](/wiki/Supporting_character "Supporting character"). She was the older sister of film director and actor [Guido Brignone](/wiki/Guido_Brignone "Guido Brignone") and the aunt of actress [Lilla Brignone](/wiki/Lilla_Brignone "Lilla Brignone").
